name: Northstar Software Delivery
description: Client-driven delivery loop with clear command, planning, execution, verification, and blocker escalation.

phases.name: Intake
phases.prompt: Restate the client goal, constraints, success bar, and any missing inputs. If the brief is partial, infer the safest high-value path and authorize work unless a hard blocker is already proven.

phases.name: Plan
phases.prompt: Turn the request into one delivery slice with owner, acceptance criteria, evidence, dependencies, and fallback. Resolve ordinary ambiguity inside the room before escalating.

phases.name: Technical Review
phases.prompt: Challenge the slice for feasibility, architecture, tooling, and hidden blockers. Tighten the execution contract before build starts and keep client escalation reserved for true hard blockers.

phases.name: Execute
phases.prompt: Build the slice, run validation, produce required evidence, and declare BLOCKER only if a true dependency, permission, or tool failure prevents responsible completion.

phases.name: Assess
phases.prompt: Assess against acceptance criteria and evidence. End with FINAL_STATUS: APPROVED, CONTINUE, or BLOCKED plus exact gaps and BLOCKER: ... Use BLOCKED only for true client or external blockers.
phases.interrupt_reason: blocked: human/client action or external dependency required

phases.name: Command
phases.prompt: If Assess returned CONTINUE, issue the next command, confirm owner, and send the room back to planning. Do not loop a repeated blocker; keep the room moving until a hard blocker is proven.
